data.text: Instagram to alert parents on teen suicide searches as UK weighs social media ban
data.snippet: Instagram said it would notify parents if their teenager repeatedly searches for terms related to suicide or self-harm within a short period, as pressure grows for governments to follow Australia's ban on the use of social media for under 16s.

image.text: A teenager poses for a photo while holding a smartphone in front of a Instagram logo in this illustration taken September 11, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Woman suing Meta, YouTube over social media addiction expected to take stand at trial
relatedHeadlines.snippet: A California woman is expected to testify in court on Thursday about how using Meta Platforms' Instagram and Google's YouTube as a child harmed her mental health, as her landmark trial in Los Angeles continues.

relatedHeadlines.text: Meta's Zuckerberg denies at LA trial that Instagram targets kids
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Wednesday repeatedly said during a landmark trial over youth social media addiction that the Facebook and Instagram operator does not allow kids under 13 on its platforms, despite being confronted with evidence suggesting they were a key demographic.

relatedHeadlines.text: Austria's top court rules Meta's ad model illegal, orders overhaul of user data practices in EU
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Austria's Supreme Court has ruled that Meta's personalised advertising model is unlawful, the court said on Thursday, requiring the company to provide EU users full access to their personal data within 14 days of a request, in a decision that sets a legal precedent across the bloc.

data.text: Berlin Film Festival director's future in spotlight after Gaza furore
data.snippet: Germany's government convened an emergency meeting of the organisers of the Berlin Film Festival on Thursday, saying it wanted to discuss the event's "direction" after debate and protests over Gaza dominated the programme.

image.text: Berlin Festival Director Tricia Tuttle attends a press conference for the movie 'No Good Men' at the 76th Berlinale International Film Festival in Berlin, Germany, February 12, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: 'DTF St. Louis' series premiere teases a love triangle turned murder mystery
relatedHeadlines.snippet: David Harbour wants audiences to know that "DTF St. Louis" isn't just a show about a dating app, messy affairs or questionable choices - it's actually about something much more universal.

data.text: Chicago honors Rev. Jesse Jackson with lineup of memorial events
data.snippet: Memorial events honoring the life and legacy of civil rights leader Jesse Jackson begin this week in Chicago, likely drawing elected officials, advocates and community members to celebrate a man whose decades-long activism reshaped American politics and amplified Black communities and communities of color.

image.text: A portrait of Rev. Jesse Jackson is displayed at the Mother AME Zion Church, on the day of a tribute to honour his life, hosted by Rev. Al Sharpton's National Action Network (NAN), in New York City, U.S., February 21, 2026.

data.text: Prince Harry trial judge says key witness can give evidence by videolink
data.snippet: A witness whose testimony could decide the outcome of a privacy lawsuit brought by Prince Harry and other high-profile figures against the Daily Mail's publisher will be allowed to give evidence remotely, the judge hearing the case ruled on Thursday.

image.text: Britain's Prince Harry arrives during the first week of a nine-week trial lawsuit against Associated Newspapers, publisher of the Daily Mail, which Britain's Prince Harry and others are suing over allegations of privacy breaches dating back 30 years, at the High Court in London, Britain, January 20, 2026.

data.text: Smartphone market set for biggest-ever decline in 2026 on memory price surge, IDC says
data.snippet: The global smartphone market is poised to suffer its biggest decline ever in 2026, sinking to a more than decade low in shipments, as surging memory chip prices drive up device costs, the International Data Corporation said on Thursday.

image.text: Customers shop for electronic products at a mall of Huaqiangbei electronics market in Shenzhen, Guangdong province, China October 30, 2025.

data.text: In prehistoric interbreeding, it was Neanderthal men and Homo sapiens women
data.snippet: When Homo sapiens trekked out of Africa, our species encountered Neanderthal populations already inhabiting the vast expanses of Europe, Asia and the Middle East. As the presence of Neanderthal DNA in most present-day people shows, interbreeding occurred, though the circumstances have remained unclear.

image.text: An AI-reconstructed image, created at Tel Aviv University, shows a mixed Neanderthal–Homo Sapiens family.

data.text: Prada layers winter jackets over light dresses at Milan show as Zuckerberg's presence fuels smart glasses talk
data.snippet: Prada's autumn/winter show in Milan on Thursday included embroidered satin dresses, sheer skirts and wool sweaters, while the front row presence of Mark Zuckerberg fuelled speculation about a potential smart glasses partnership.

image.text: A model presents a creation from the Prada Fall/Winter 2026/2027 collection during Fashion Week in Milan, Italy, February 26, 2026.

data.text: Armani heirs say new Emporio collection blends continuity and 'sparkling' touch
data.snippet: The heirs of Giorgio Armani — his partner Leo Dell'Orco and his niece Silvana Armani — said they "had fun" working together on the Emporio Armani collection, which stays true to the late designer's style while introducing some new touches.

image.text: Nicola Lamorgese, Marco Brunello, Silvana Armani and Leo Dell'Orco attend the Emporio Armani Fall/Winter 2026/2027 collection show during Fashion Week in Milan, Italy, February 26, 2026.

data.text: Korean director Park Chan-wook to lead Cannes jury
data.snippet: Award-winning Korean director Park Chan-wook will preside over the jury of the 79th Cannes Film Festival, the organisers said on Thursday.

image.text: Park Chan-wook attends the 2026 BAFTA Tea Party in Los Angeles, California, U.S., January 10, 2026.

data.text: INSIGHT-Spain's looming migrant amnesty strains services, sends applicants scrambling
data.snippet: Spain's newest drive to fast-track legal status for at least half a million undocumented migrants has already burdened immigration offices and sparked anxiety among prospective applicants weeks before the process even begins, a dozen union officials, lawyers, and migrants said.

image.text: Alexandra Benites, 47, and her daughter Carla Benites, 17, from Ecuador, pose with their passports at St. Augustine's church, also known as the "Church of the Migrants", in Barcelona's Raval district, Spain February 25, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Spain to grant around half a million undocumented migrants legal status
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Spain's Socialist-led government presented a draft decree on Tuesday to expedite legal status to hundreds of thousands of undocumented immigrants, in its latest move to integrate migrant workers even as other European countries curb immigration.

data.text: 'Blessed rain': Japanese men in loincloths  wrestle in harvest rite
data.snippet: More than 30 Japanese men clad in loincloths braved cold and heavy rain in a harvest festival more than two centuries old, held in a small, muddy field in a residential pocket outside Tokyo, the capital.

image.text: Participants in loincloths wrestle in a mud field during Warabi Hadaka Matsuri (Warabi Naked Festival) in Yotsukaido, Chiba Prefecture, Japan, February 25, 2026. According to organisers, the festival, which has about 200 years of history, is held in hopes of a bountiful harvest.

data.text: Vatican unveils unusual postage stamp honouring Ukraine's Catholics
data.snippet: The Vatican unveiled a new postage stamp on Thursday honouring Ukraine's Catholics, with the design showing their cathedral in Kyiv during a blackout in an unusually pointed reference to the daily struggles of Ukrainians in wartime.

image.text: A newly issued Vatican postage stamp honouring Catholics in Ukraine is pasted on an envelope at the Vatican, February 26, 2026.

data.text: Norway's King Harald to be discharged from hospital in Spain
data.snippet: Norway's King Harald, 89, who fell ill while on holiday in Spain, will be discharged from hospital on Thursday, the royal palace said in a statement.

image.text: Norway's King Harald and Queen Sonja attend the ceremony to award the Nobel Peace Prize to Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado, in Oslo, Norway December 10, 2025.

data.text: Congress to weigh extending space station life, NASA moon base
data.snippet: A U.S. Senate committee next week will consider extending the planned life of the International Space Station by two years to give companies more time to develop a replacement, one of a few changes to a NASA bill focused on rivaling China's growing footprint in space.

image.text: The NASA logo hangs in the Mission Operations Control Center at Wallops Flight Facility on Wallops Island, Virginia, U.S., October 26, 2022.

relatedHeadlines.text: NASA may take moon mission Artemis II rocket back to assembly site, affecting March launch window
relatedHeadlines.snippet: NASA is taking steps to potentially roll back the Artemis II rocket and Orion spacecraft to its Vehicle Assembly Building after observing an interrupted flow of helium, the space agency said on Saturday. "This will almost assuredly impact the March launch window," NASA said.

relatedHeadlines.text: ANALYSIS-Musk fires up SpaceX, Bezos pushes Blue Origin as US billionaires race China to moon
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The space race between U.S. billionaires is heating up, with Elon Musk's SpaceX planning to build a lunar base and Jeff Bezos pushing Blue Origin's ambitions as both companies aim to return humans to the moon ahead of a planned mission by China in 2030.

data.text: Fiji and Tuvalu to host pre-COP31 climate meetings
data.snippet: Fiji and Tuvalu will host key meetings in the lead-up to the COP31 climate summit in 2026, the Pacific Islands Forum said on Thursday.

image.text: The morning's first rays of sunlight hit the island community of Serua Village, Fiji, July 15, 2022.

relatedHeadlines.text: Climate shocks threaten $2.3 trillion global sports economy, study warns
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Extreme weather threatens annual revenue growth in the $2.3 trillion sports economy, where expansion is driven mainly by tourism tied to resource-depleting global events such as the just-concluded Milano Cortina Games, a report has shown.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
